metadata.description:
Batch-fix 15 remaining test failures on fix/fda-readiness-improvement-xml using proper GATHER→CATEGORIZE→PLAN→FIX→VERIFY methodology (Rule #23). Also: improve self-learning-iterative-coder skill to prevent whac-a-mole.
METALEARNING REFERENCE: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md CLAUDE.md Rule #23: NEVER Fix Failures Serially
metadata.prior-work: Phase 1-4 of fda-insights-second-pass-executable.xml COMPLETE. OpenLineage wired into 5 flows, CycloneDX SBOM added, KG updated, 5 GitHub issues created. 4614 tests pass, 15 remain.
metadata.cold-start-instructions: 1. git checkout fix/fda-readiness-improvement-xml 2. git pull 3. Read this XML plan 4. Execute Phase 1 (batch fix), then Phase 2 (skill improvement) 5. Run make test-staging — must be ZERO failures 6. git push 7. Wait for user command before creating PR

root-cause.name: slash-prefix-metric-keys
root-cause.description: MLflow slash-prefix migration (#790) changed metric/param key format from underscore (eval_fold0_dsc) to slash (eval/fold0/dsc). Tests still assert old underscore keys. This is the SAME root cause that affected test_system_info.py (19 tests) and test_observability.py (10+ tests) — already fixed in earlier commits on this branch.

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_trainer_metrics.py
file.failure: train_dice → train/dice in logged metrics set

root-cause.fix-strategy: Batch sed-like replacement of underscore keys to slash keys in all 3 test files. Use ast.parse to verify syntax after replacement. Do NOT replace function names or variable names — only string literals used as dict keys or assertion targets.

root-cause.name: checkpoint-naming-changed
root-cause.description: Trainer refactor changed checkpoint naming from best_val_loss.pth to a different convention. 6 tests in test_trainer_multi_metric.py expect the old best_*.pth pattern. The trainer now uses a different naming scheme for checkpoints (possibly metric-based subdirectories or different filename patterns).

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_trainer_multi_metric.py
file.failure: test_early_stop_triggered: Expected early stop before epoch 20

root-cause.fix-strategy: 1. Read the actual Trainer.fit() code to understand current checkpoint naming convention 2. Update ALL 6 tests to match the actual convention 3. For early_stop_triggered: check if early stopping logic changed 4. Single commit for all 6 fixes

root-cause.name: skypilot-yaml-restructured
root-cause.description: SkyPilot YAML files were restructured (DVC remote strategy change, timing instrumentation). Tests expect DVC_REMOTE env var and dvc_pull timing markers that may have been moved or renamed.

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_skypilot_configs.py
file.failure: smoke_test_gpu.yaml setup must include dvc pull -r remote_storage

root-cause.fix-strategy: 1. Read the actual SkyPilot YAML files to see current structure 2. Update tests to match actual YAML content 3. If DVC_REMOTE was intentionally removed, update test expectations 4. Single commit for all 4 fixes

phase.name: batch-fix-remaining-failures
phase.methodology: Rule #23 — GATHER → CATEGORIZE → PLAN → FIX → VERIFY

step.name: GATHER
step.command: MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 PREFECT_DISABLED=1 uv run pytest tests/ \ -m "not model_loading and not slow and not integration and not gpu and not gpu_heavy" \ --ignore=tests/gpu_instance --ignore=tests/v2/cloud \ -q --tb=line --maxfail=200 -p no:warnings 2>&1 | grep "FAILED"
step.acceptance: Exactly 15 failures matching the inventory above.

step.name: FIX-RC1: Slash-prefix metric keys (5 tests)
step.approach: For each file: 1. Read the file 2. Identify ALL string literals that are dict keys with underscore format (eval_fold0_dsc, prof_first_epoch_seconds, train_dice, etc.) 3. Map each to the slash-prefix equivalent using the MetricKeys class in src/minivess/observability/metric_keys.py as the source of truth 4. Replace ALL at once (not one-by-one) 5. Verify syntax with ast.parse() 6. Run the specific test file to confirm GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: batch slash-prefix migration for tracker_evaluation, timing, trainer_metrics (#790)

step.name: FIX-RC2: Checkpoint naming (6 tests)
step.approach: 1. Read src/minivess/pipeline/trainer.py to find the ACTUAL checkpoint saving code — what filename pattern does it use? 2. Read all 6 failing test methods to understand what they expect 3. Update ALL 6 tests to match the actual trainer behavior 4. For early_stop_triggered: check if patience/threshold changed 5. Run test_trainer_multi_metric.py to confirm all 6 GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: update checkpoint naming assertions to match refactored trainer

step.name: FIX-RC3: SkyPilot YAML content (4 tests)
step.approach: 1. Read the actual SkyPilot YAML files 2. Check if DVC_REMOTE env var exists or was renamed/removed 3. Check if dvc_pull timing markers exist 4. Update tests to match reality OR add missing YAML content 5. Run all 3 test files to confirm GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: align SkyPilot YAML tests with current deployment configs

step.name: PUSH
step.note: Do NOT create PR — wait for user command.

phase.name: improve-tdd-skill
phase.issue: P0 — must be done on this branch before PR
phase.description:
Two compounding systemic failures keep recurring in this project:
BUG-1 (SILENT DISMISSAL): Claude Code encounters a test failure, classifies it as "pre-existing" or "not related to current changes", and moves on. The failure persists across sessions. Nobody creates an issue. Nobody fixes it. 8 documented instances in metalearning/2026-03-07-silent-existing-failures.md. The phrase "not related to current changes" is BANNED per CLAUDE.md Rule #20.
BUG-2 (WHAC-A-MOLE): When multiple test failures exist, Claude Code fixes them one at a time — run tests, fix one failure, run tests, fix next failure. This takes 25+ minutes for what should be a 10-minute batch operation. Documented in metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md. BANNED per CLAUDE.md Rule #23.
Both bugs must be prevented by encoding the correct behavior INTO the self-learning-iterative-coder skill protocols so they are automatically followed in every future session, without the user having to remind.
The skill improvement is the COMPOUNDING fix — it prevents both bugs from ever recurring, making every future session more efficient.

step.name: Add failure-triage protocol (fixes BUG-1 + BUG-2)
step.content-outline:
# Failure Triage Protocol
## Fixes TWO systemic bugs: - BUG-1: Silent dismissal of "pre-existing" failures (Rule #20) - BUG-2: Whac-a-mole serial failure fixing (Rule #23)
## TRIGGER: After ANY test suite run that shows failures
## ZERO TOLERANCE RULE (BUG-1 prevention): "Pre-existing" is NOT a valid classification. "Not related to current changes" is BANNED. "Separate issue" without creating the issue in the SAME response is a lie. EVERY failure MUST result in: (a) fixed, (b) issue created, or (c) reported. There are no orphan failures. Period.
## MANDATORY STEPS (BUG-2 prevention):
### Step 1: GATHER (one pass, no stopping) Command: pytest --maxfail=200 WITHOUT -x NEVER use -x for failure investigation. Capture ALL failures.
### Step 2: CATEGORIZE (group by root cause, not by test) Command: grep "FAILED" | sed 's/::.*//g' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn Then read failure messages. One root cause often explains 50+ failures. Output: table with columns [Root Cause | Files | Count | Fix Strategy]
### Step 3: PLAN (one fix per root cause, not per test) For each root cause: - Identify the SOURCE OF TRUTH (e.g., MetricKeys for key names, Trainer.fit() for checkpoint naming, actual YAML for config) - Plan a batch replacement/fix that handles ALL instances - Estimate total test count that will turn green
### Step 4: FIX (batch, one commit per root cause) - Fix ALL instances of each root cause in ONE pass - ast.parse() every modified file to verify no syntax corruption - Run ONLY the affected test files to confirm GREEN - ONE commit per root cause, not per test
### Step 5: VERIFY (full suite again, WITHOUT -x) Run the full staging suite. If new failures appear, go to Step 1. Do NOT start fixing new failures immediately — triage them first.
## BANNED ANTI-PATTERNS: - Running with -x and fixing one test at a time (whac-a-mole) - Committing after each individual test fix - Saying "pre-existing" or "not related to current changes" - Using replace_all on strings without checking Python identifiers - Proceeding to next plan task while failures exist - Creating a "separate issue" without actually creating it
## ESCALATION: - 10+ files with same root cause → launch an Agent for batch replacement - 3+ root causes → write a mini XML plan before fixing anything - Same failure seen in 2+ sessions → escalate to metalearning doc

step.name: Update SKILL.md Critical Rules + Protocol Reference
step.changes:
1. Add to Critical Rules section (after Rule #5): "### 9. ZERO TOLERANCE FOR OBSERVED FAILURES (Rule #20) Every test failure seen during a session MUST result in: fixed, issue created, or reported to user. 'Pre-existing' is NOT a classification. 'Not related to current changes' is BANNED. See: metalearning/2026-03-07-silent-existing-failures.md"
2. Add to Critical Rules section (after Rule #9): "### 10. FAILURE TRIAGE BEFORE FIXING (Rule #23) When the VERIFY phase reveals failures, STOP. Do NOT proceed to fix-phase. Invoke the Failure Triage Protocol (failure-triage.md) to GATHER all failures, CATEGORIZE by root cause, PLAN batch fixes, then FIX. Never fix one failure at a time. See: metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md"
3. Add to Protocol Reference table: | Failure Triage | Handle multiple failures systematically | protocols/failure-triage.md |
4. Add to Anti-Patterns table: | Silent dismissal | Classifying failures as "pre-existing" | Zero tolerance — fix, issue, or report | | Whac-a-mole | Fixing one test at a time in a loop | Failure Triage Protocol |

step.name: Update verify-phase protocol
step.change:
Add MANDATORY gate at the top of the verify phase: "## FAILURE GATE (MANDATORY) If this verification run reveals ANY failures: 1. Do NOT proceed to fix-phase 2. Invoke protocols/failure-triage.md FIRST 3. The triage protocol will plan batch fixes by root cause 4. Only after triage is complete, proceed to fix-phase
NEVER fix failures one at a time. This is the #1 time-wasting anti-pattern (metalearning 2026-03-18).
NEVER dismiss failures as 'pre-existing'. Every failure in this repo was co-authored by Claude Code (metalearning 2026-03-07)."

step.name: Update fix-phase protocol
step.change:
Add MANDATORY pre-check at the top: "## PRE-CHECK: Did you run Failure Triage? Before fixing any failure, verify: [ ] Failure Triage Protocol was executed (failure-triage.md) [ ] ALL failures gathered (not just the first one) [ ] Root causes categorized (table exists) [ ] Fix plan exists (one fix per root cause, not per test)
If ANY check is NO → STOP. Go to failure-triage.md.
## PRE-CHECK: Is this a 'pre-existing' failure? The phrase 'pre-existing' is BANNED. The phrase 'not related to current changes' is BANNED. Every failure must be: (a) Fixed in this session, OR (b) GitHub issue created with root cause + priority, OR (c) Explicitly reported to user with recommendation
'Separate issue' without creating the issue is a LIE."

step.name: Update ACTIVATION-CHECKLIST.md
step.change:
Add to Section 2 (Project Setup) after "baseline is green" check: "**If baseline is NOT green:** Do NOT skip failures. Run the Failure Triage Protocol (protocols/failure-triage.md) to categorize and fix ALL existing failures before entering the TDD loop. Every failure you ignore now will cost 10x more to fix later.
The phrase 'fix existing issues first' does NOT mean 'ignore them and move on'. It means: create issues, fix what you can, document the rest."

step.name: Commit skill improvements
step.commit-message:
chore(skill): add failure-triage protocol + zero-tolerance rules
Addresses TWO systemic bugs in the self-learning-iterative-coder skill:
BUG-1 (silent dismissal): Added Zero Tolerance rule (Rule #20 enforcement). Every failure must be fixed, issued, or reported. "Pre-existing" is BANNED. Metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-07-silent-existing-failures.md
BUG-2 (whac-a-mole): Added Failure Triage Protocol with GATHER→CATEGORIZE→PLAN→FIX→VERIFY methodology. Rule #23 enforcement. Metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md
Updated: SKILL.md (2 new rules, 2 new anti-patterns, protocol ref), verify-phase.md (failure gate), fix-phase.md (pre-checks), ACTIVATION-CHECKLIST.md (baseline failure handling).

success-criteria.criterion: All 15 failures fixed in exactly 3 commits (one per root cause)
failure-triage.md protocol exists and covers BOTH BUG-1 and BUG-2
SKILL.md has Rules #9 (zero tolerance) and #10 (triage before fixing)
SKILL.md Anti-Patterns table has "silent dismissal" and "whac-a-mole"
verify-phase.md has FAILURE GATE at the top
fix-phase.md has PRE-CHECK for triage completion
NO PR created (wait for user command)
